economic_notes: GBEs include LBB Gas Bar & Convenience LP, LBB Coffee Franchise LP, Wakayos Developments LP, Wakayos Holdings Inc, Wakayos Employment & Training Center Inc, DS-LBB Holdings Inc, and related general partner companies. Wakayos Holdings Inc suffered large losses of $1,128,475. DS-LBB Holdings Inc swung from $495,752 surplus to ($72,199) loss position.
notes: Fiscal year end is March 31, 2023 (not 2024). Nine GBEs accounted for under modified equity method including gas bar, coffee franchise, development and holdings companies. Net debt position of $10.8M. Large reserve for losses in GBEs of $1.89M. Deferred revenue (unexpended funds) of $2.17M treated as liability. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $4,231,554 + Trust funds $93,101 + CMHC subsidy $12,454 + FSIN $46,833 + File Hills Community Health $12,333 + FHQ Tribal Council $330,602 + Yorkton Tribal Council CFS $269,214 + Living Sky CDC $7,317 + First Nations Trust $228,119 = $5,231,527 (approximate; FNT and Living Sky may not be government transfers). OSR includes land leases, GBE losses, rental income, insurance proceeds, other revenue. Schedule of remuneration not included in extracted text. Long-term debt note was cut off in source text.
